author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John viii. 31, ‘If ye abide in my word, then are ye truly my disciples,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John viii. 31, ‘If ye abide in my word, then are ye truly my disciples,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye know well, Beloved, that we all have One Master, and are fellow disciples under Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nor are we your masters, because we speak to you from this higher spot; but He is the Master of all, who dwelleth in us all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He just now spake to us all in the Gospel, and said to us, what I also am saying to you; but He saith it of us, as well of us as of you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If ye shall continue in My word,” not of course in my word who am now speaking to you; but in His who spake just now out of the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If ye shall continue in My word,” saith He, “ye are My disciples indeed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To be a disciple, it is not enough to come, but to continue.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He doth not therefore say, “If ye shall hear My word;” or, “If ye shall come to My word;” or, “If ye shall praise My word;” but observe what He said, “If ye shall continue in My word, ye are My disciples indeed, and ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall free you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What shall we say, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To continue in the word of God, is it toilsome, or is it not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If it be toilsome, look at the great reward; if it be not toilsome, thou receivest the reward for nought.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Continue we then in Him who continueth in us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We, if we continue not in Him, fall; but He if He continue not in us, hath not on that account lost an habitation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He skilleth to continue in Himself, who never leaveth Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But for man, God forbid that he should continue in himself who hath lost himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then we continue in Him through indigence; He continueth in us through mercy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then seeing it hath been set forth what we ought to do, let us see what we are to receive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He hath appointed a work, and promised a reward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the work?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If ye shall continue in Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A short work; short in description, great in execution.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If ye shall build on the Rock.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O how great a thing is this, Brethren, to build on the Rock, how great is it!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The floods came, the winds blew, the rain descended, and beat upon that house, and it fell not; for it was founded upon a rock.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is to continue in the word of God, but not to yield to any temptations?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The reward, what is it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall free you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Bear with me, for ye perceive that my voice is feeble; assist me by your calm attention.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Glorious reward!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Ye shall know the truth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here one may haply say, “And what doth it profit me to know the truth?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And the truth shall free you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the truth have no charms for you, let freedom have its charms.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the usage of the Latin tongue, the expression, “to be free,” is used in two senses; and chiefly we are accustomed to hear this word in this sense, that whosoever is free may be understood to escape some danger, to be rid of some embarrassment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the proper signification of “to be free,” is “to be made free;” just as “to be saved,” is “to be made safe;” “to be healed,” is, “to be made whole;” so “to be freed,” is “to be made free.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore I said, “If the truth have no charms for you, let freedom have its charms.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is expressed more evidently in the Greek language, nor can it be there understood in any other sense.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that ye may know that in no other sense can it be understood; when the Lord spake, the Jews answered, “We were never in bondage to any man; how sayest thou the Truth shall free you?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That is, “the Truth shall make you free,” how sayest thou to us, who were never in bondage to any man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “How,” say they, “dost Thou promise them freedom, who as Thou seest never bare the hard yoke of bondage?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They heard what they ought; but they did not what they ought.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What did they hear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because I said, “The truth shall free you;” ye turned your thoughts upon yourselves, that ye are not in bondage to man, and ye said, “We were never in bondage to any man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Every one,” Jew and Greek, rich and poor, the man in authority and private station, the emperor and the beggar, “Every one that committeth sin is the servant of sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Every one,” saith He, “that committeth sin is the servant of sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If men but acknowledge their bondage, they will see from whence they may obtain freedom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some free-born man has been taken captive by the barbarians, from a free man is made a slave; another hears, and pities him, considers how that he has money, becomes his ransomer, goes to the barbarians, gives money, ransoms the man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he has indeed restored freedom, if he have taken away iniquity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what man has ever taken away iniquity from another man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who was in bondage with the barbarians, has been redeemed by his ransomer; and great difference there is between the ransomer and the ransomed; yet haply are they fellow-slaves under the lordship of iniquity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask him that was ransomed, “Hast thou sin?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I have,” he says.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask the ransomer, “Hast thou sin?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I have,” he says.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then neither do thou boast thyself that thou hast been ransomed, nor thou uplift thyself that thou art his ransomer; but fly both of you to the True Deliverer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is but a small part of it, that they who are under sin, are called servants; they are even called dead; what a man is afraid of captivity bringing upon him, iniquity has brought on him already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what? because they seem to be alive, was He then mistaken who said, “Let the dead bury their dead”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then all under sin are dead, dead servants, dead in their service, servants in their death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who then freeth from death and from bondage, save He, who is “Free among the dead”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is “Free among the dead,” save He who among sinners is without sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lo, the prince of the world cometh,” saith our Redeemer Himself, our Deliverer, “Lo, the prince of the world cometh, and shall find nothing in Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He holds fast those whom he hath deceived, whom he hath seduced, whom he hath persuaded to sin and death; “in Me shall he find nothing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Come, Lord, Redeemer come, come; let the captive acknowledge thee, him that leadeth captive flee thee; be Thou my Deliverer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lost as I was, He hath found me in Whom the devil findeth nothing that cometh of the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The prince of this world findeth in Him Flesh, he findeth it but what kind of Flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A mortal Flesh, which he can seize, which he can crucify, which he can kill.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art mistaken, O deceiver, the Redeemer is not deceived; thou art mistaken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou seest in the Lord a mortal Flesh, it is not flesh of sin, it is the likeness of flesh of sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For God sent His Son in the likeness of flesh of sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: True Flesh, mortal Flesh; but not flesh of sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For God sent His Son in the likeness of flesh of sin, that by sin He might condemn sin in the Flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For God sent His Son in the likeness of flesh of sin;” in Flesh, but not in flesh of sin; but “in the likeness of flesh of sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what purpose?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That by sin,” of which assuredly there was none in Him, “He might condemn sin in the flesh; that the righteousness of the Law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then it was “the likeness of flesh of sin,” not flesh of sin, how, “That by sin He might condemn sin in the flesh”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So a likeness is wont to receive the name of that thing of which it is a likeness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The word man is used for a real man; but if you show a man painted on the wall, and enquire what it is, it is answered, “A man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then Flesh having the likeness of flesh of sin, that it might be a sacrifice for sin, is called “sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The same Apostle says in another place, “He made Him to be sin for us, who knew no sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Him who knew no sin:” Who is He who knew no sin, but He That said, “Behold the prince of the world cometh, and shall find nothing in me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Him who knew no sin, made He sin for us;” even Christ Himself, who knew no sin, God made sin for us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does this mean, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If it were said, “He made sin upon Him,” or, “He made Him to have sin;” it would seem intolerable; how do we tolerate what is said, “He made Him sin,” that Christ Himself should be sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who are acquainted with the Scriptures of the Old Testament recognise what I am saying.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not an expression once used, but repeatedly, very constantly, sacrifices for sins are called “sins.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A goat, for instance, was offered for sin, a ram, anything; the victim itself which was offered for sin was called “sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A sacrifice for sin then was called “sin;” so that in one place the Law says, “That the Priests are to lay their hands upon the sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Him” then, “who knew no sin, He made sin for us;” that is, “He was made a sacrifice for sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Sin was offered, and sin was cancelled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Blood of the Redeemer was shed, and the debtor’s bond was cancelled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the “Blood, That was shed for many for the remission of sins.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What meaneth this then thy senseless exultation, O thou that didst hold me captive, for that my Deliverer had mortal Flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, if He had sin; if thou hast found anything of thine in Him, hold Him fast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Word was made Flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Word is the Creator, the Flesh His creature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is there here of thine, O enemy?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Word is God, and His Human Soul is His creature, and His Human Flesh His creature, and the Mortal Flesh of God is His creature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seek for sin here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what art thou seeking?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Truth saith, “The prince of this world shall come, and shall find nothing in Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not therefore not find Flesh, but nothing of his own, that is, no sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou didst deceive the innocent, thou madest them guilty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou didst slay the Innocent; thou destroyedst Him from whom thou hadst nothing due, render back what thou didst hold fast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then didst thou exult for a short hour, because thou didst find in Christ mortal Flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was thy trap: whereupon thou didst rejoice, thereby hast thou been taken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherein thou didst exult that thou hadst found something, therein thou sorrowest now that thou hast lost what thou didst possess.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, let us who believe in Christ, continue in His word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if we shall continue in His word, we are His disciples indeed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For not those twelve only, but all we who continue in His word are His disciples indeed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And “we shall know the Truth, and the Truth shall free us;” that is, Christ the Son of God who hath said, “I am the Truth,” shall make you free, that is, shall free you, not from barbarians, but from the devil; not from the captivity of the body, but from the iniquity of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is He Only who freeth in such wise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one call himself free, lest he remain a slave.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our soul shall not remain in bondage, for that day by day our debts are forgiven.
